title: The Angel visits Mary. Nativity part 8
scripture_text: ‘’In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a village in Galilee, to a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of King David. Gabriel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, favoured woman! The Lord is with you! Confused and disturbed, Mary tried to think what the angel could mean. “Don’t be afraid, Mary,” the angel told her, “for you have found favour with God. You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus. He will be very great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his ancestor David. And he will reign over Israel forever; his Kingdom will never end!” Mary asked the angel, “But how can this happen? I am a virgin.” the angel replied, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the baby to be born will be holy, and he will be called the Son of God. What’s more, your relative Elizabeth has become pregnant in her old age! People used to say she was barren, but she has conceived a son and is now in her sixth month. For the word of God will never fail.” Mary responded, “I am the Lord’s servant. May everything you have said about me come true.” And then the angel left her.’’
devotional:
Sometimes in life we can get some really good news, and we get excited and feel really encouraged. However, sometimes good news can challenge and confront us, it can threaten our status-quo. For example if you were to hear that you were to be getting a new job with a large pay increase, you would be thrilled, but you may also feel a bit daunted because you will have new and probably more responsibilities.

The Good news is, that Jesus was to be born of Mary.

Jesus, the creator of the universe became a tiny cluster of cells in a womb of a virgin. As God stepped down, Mary began to carry His greatest expression of love for the world in her womb.

‘It is by far the most amazing miracle in the whole Bible – far more amazing than the resurrection and more amazing than the creation of the universe. The fact that the infinite, omnipotent, eternal Son of God could become man and join Himself to a human nature forever, so that infinite God became one person with finite man, will remain for eternity the most profound miracle and the most profound mystery in all the universe.’ Wayne Grudem

The Word became flesh! God became human! The invisible became visible! The untouchable became touchable! Eternal life experienced temporal death! The transcendent one, descended and drew near! The unlimited became limited! The infinite became finite! The immutable became mutable! The unbreakable became fragile! Spirit became matter! Eternity entered time! The independent became dependent! The almighty became weak! The loved became hated! The exalted was humbled! Glory was subjected to shame! Fame turned into obscurity! From inexpressible joy to tears of unimaginable grief! From a throne to a cross! From ruler to being ruled! From power to weakness! Sam Storms. Pleasures Evermore 2000, p. 153-154.

We should be people who rejoice and glory in the incarnation of Jesus, we have a God who understands us, a God who identifies with us, a God who cares for us, a God who is with us, this is very good news.

However, the good news of Jesus’ birth is also challenging and confronting. It will confront and challenge our understanding of life and how we live it. We need to grasp the fact that the nativity story needs to become part of our story. I understand that the nativity story as recorded in the bible is unique and can never be repeated. However, the spiritual principles and biblical truths that shaped Mary life, need to shape our lives. The birth narratives in the gospels are more than historical records, they are also templates God uses to continually reveal Jesus. We must learn from Mary’s relationship with God, having the expected hope that Jesus will be formed in us. We all need our own personalised ‘nativity’ story. Our lives need to become an echo of the nativity, where Jesus is formed in us and revealed through us. Let’s be honest we would rather watch the nativity as a sanitised children’s play than be part of it. The bold allegiance that the original characters of the nativity gave to God, needs to become ours. So let us look at our passage and try and become a people in whom the life of Jesus can be produced in us.
prayer: Father may my life echo the nativity story, may Jesus be formed in me.
html:
The Angel visits Mary. Nativity part 8

Study Text: Luke 1:26-38
‘’In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a village in Galilee, to a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of King David. Gabriel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, favoured woman! The Lord is with you! Confused and disturbed, Mary tried to think what the angel could mean. “Don’t be afraid, Mary,” the angel told her, “for you have found favour with God. You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus. He will be very great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his ancestor David. And he will reign over Israel forever; his Kingdom will never end!” Mary asked the angel, “But how can this happen? I am a virgin.” the angel replied, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the baby to be born will be holy, and he will be called the Son of God. What’s more, your relative Elizabeth has become pregnant in her old age! People used to say she was barren, but she has conceived a son and is now in her sixth month. For the word of God will never fail.” Mary responded, “I am the Lord’s servant. May everything you have said about me come true.” And then the angel left her.’’

Sometimes in life we can get some really good news, and we get excited and feel really encouraged. However, sometimes good news can challenge and confront us, it can threaten our status-quo. For example if you were to hear that you were to be getting a new job with a large pay increase, you would be thrilled, but you may also feel a bit daunted because you will have new and probably more responsibilities.

The Good news is, that Jesus was to be born of Mary.

Jesus, the creator of the universe became a tiny cluster of cells in a womb of a virgin. As God stepped down, Mary began to carry His greatest expression of love for the world in her womb.

‘It is by far the most amazing miracle in the whole Bible – far more amazing than the resurrection and more amazing than the creation of the universe. The fact that the infinite, omnipotent, eternal Son of God could become man and join Himself to a human nature forever, so that infinite God became one person with finite man, will remain for eternity the most profound miracle and the most profound mystery in all the universe.’ Wayne Grudem

The Word became flesh! God became human! The invisible became visible! The untouchable became touchable! Eternal life experienced temporal death! The transcendent one, descended and drew near! The unlimited became limited! The infinite became finite! The immutable became mutable! The unbreakable became fragile! Spirit became matter! Eternity entered time! The independent became dependent! The almighty became weak! The loved became hated! The exalted was humbled! Glory was subjected to shame! Fame turned into obscurity! From inexpressible joy to tears of unimaginable grief! From a throne to a cross! From ruler to being ruled! From power to weakness! Sam Storms. Pleasures Evermore 2000, p. 153-154.

We should be people who rejoice and glory in the incarnation of Jesus, we have a God who understands us, a God who identifies with us, a God who cares for us, a God who is with us, this is very good news.

However, the good news of Jesus’ birth is also challenging and confronting. It will confront and challenge our understanding of life and how we live it. We need to grasp the fact that the nativity story needs to become part of our story. I understand that the nativity story as recorded in the bible is unique and can never be repeated. However, the spiritual principles and biblical truths that shaped Mary life, need to shape our lives. The birth narratives in the gospels are more than historical records, they are also templates God uses to continually reveal Jesus. We must learn from Mary’s relationship with God, having the expected hope that Jesus will be formed in us. We all need our own personalised ‘nativity’ story. Our lives need to become an echo of the nativity, where Jesus is formed in us and revealed through us. Let’s be honest we would rather watch the nativity as a sanitised children’s play than be part of it. The bold allegiance that the original characters of the nativity gave to God, needs to become ours. So let us look at our passage and try and become a people in whom the life of Jesus can be produced in us.

Prayer: Father may my life echo the nativity story, may Jesus be formed in me.
Bible in a year reading plan: Ezra 8-10
